overview.short: Ghargad Fort / Gadgada Fort (Marathi: घरगड किल्ला) is a fort located 145 km from Mumbai, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Ghargad Fort / Gadgada Fort (Marathi: घरगड किल्ला) is a fort located 145 km from Mumbai, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra. This fort is less visited fort in Nashik district. This fort is quite difficult to climb.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jategaon
topPlaces.description: Jategaon is a village in Nashik taluka in Nashik district of state of Maharashtra, India. Jategaon belongs to Madhe Maharashtra and Northern Maharashtra region and Nashik Division.

topPlaces.name: Waldevi Dam
topPlaces.description: Waldevi Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Waldevi River near Nashik in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Bahula Fort
topPlaces.description: Bahula fort is located in the Igatpuri taluka of Nashik district. It is located on the ancient trade route passing through Thal ghat.

topPlaces.name: Anjneri Dam
topPlaces.description: Anjneri Dam, is an earthfill dam on Panzara River near Sakri in Nashik district of state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Mukane Dam
topPlaces.description: Mukane Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Aaundha river near Igatpuri, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Anjaneri
topPlaces.description: Anjaneri, one of the forts in the mountain range of Nasik-Trimbakeshwa. Anjaneri is located 20 km away from Nasik by Trimbak Road.

topPlaces.name: Mahiravani
topPlaces.description: Mahiravani is a historical village in Nashik district of Maharashtra state of India. It is on the way from Nashik to Trimbakeshwar.

items.note: Jategaon is a village in Nashik taluka in Nashik district of state of Maharashtra, India. Jategaon belongs to Madhe Maharashtra and Northern Maharashtra region and Nashik Division.
Waldevi Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Waldevi River near Nashik in the state of Maharashtra in India.
Bahula fort is located in the Igatpuri taluka of Nashik district. It is located on the ancient trade route passing through Thal ghat.
Anjneri Dam, is an earthfill dam on Panzara River near Sakri in Nashik district of state of Maharashtra in India.
Mukane Dam, is an earthfill dam on the Aaundha river near Igatpuri, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.
Anjaneri, one of the forts in the mountain range of Nasik-Trimbakeshwa. Anjaneri is located 20 km away from Nasik by Trimbak Road.
Mahiravani is a historical village in Nashik district of Maharashtra state of India. It is on the way from Nashik to Trimbakeshwar.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ghargad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ghargad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (80 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (22 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ghargad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ghargad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ghargad?
faq.a: Stays in Ghargad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ghargad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jategaon, Waldevi Dam, Bahula Fort, Anjneri Dam, Mukane Dam.
